action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 27 test had typo (lpip_vals). The eval in iter 26 failed with shape mismatch (65526 vs 65536) even though validate_rays.py passed all val objects. This suggests that the current predict.py's manual ray generation may have an edge-case bug, most likely the gimbal lock when elevation approaches ±90° (lookat parallel to up → cross product zero → NaN rays). Analysis: We need to make the ray generation robust to all camera poses by using a fallback up vector when the look direction is nearly parallel to the world up. We'll write a comprehensive test that (1) uses a robust ray generation function with fallback, (2) tests it on a subset of val objects to compute LPIPS and ensure quality, (3) tests all 100 val objects for crashes and shape mismatches. If the robust version works and LPIPS remains similar to our earlier test (~0.574), we will update predict.py and submit eval in the next iteration (avoiding wasting an iteration on a likely-failing eval). Decision: Write test_robust_rays_fixed.py with robust ray generation, run it to validate on 100 val objects, and report LPIPS on a few. Then decide on eval submission.
